data.body:
Implemented in src/rebar/_snapshot/ (new importable package). DEVIATION from the ticket's illustrative 'src/rebar/_engine/repo_snapshot.py': src/rebar/_engine/ is package DATA (subprocess tooling reached via engine_dir()), NOT importable — rebar._engine resolves to the _engine.py MODULE — so a module there could not be imported by the gates (S3). Chose the importable rebar._snapshot package instead; behavioral ACs unaffected.

repo_snapshot.py: materialize(ref, *, source_mode) -> SnapshotHandle. attested = coalesced 'git fetch origin' (--filter=blob:none, GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0) -> resolve to immutable SHA -> faithful 'git read-tree <sha>' into a throwaway GIT_INDEX_FILE + 'git checkout-index --all --prefix' -> atomic build-in-tmp + rename into content-addressed <root>/<sha> + fsync. local = in-place checkout, never signable.

AC mapping: AC1 export-ignore present + export-subst NOT applied (faithful, byte-match); AC2 LFS pointers detected via magic header + submodule gitlinks (mode 160000) omitted+recorded, both persisted in a sidecar caveats file so cross-process cache hits stay correct; AC3 atomic rename + sweep_tmp() startup recovery; AC4 no external tar, REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR/gettempdir (no hardcoded /tmp), fcntl.flock + atomic-mkdir fallback; AC5 descriptive SnapshotFetchError credential remedy, attested fails closed; AC6 throwaway GIT_INDEX_FILE so different-SHA materializations never touch repo index.lock (proven by status-unchanged concurrency test).

Subagent code review found + I FIXED a real command-injection (RCE): the targeted-fetch path appended a client ref to 'git fetch' without --end-of-options, so a ref like '--upload-pack=<cmd>' would execute. Fixed (mirrors the --end-of-options guard already in rev-parse) + regression test test_malicious_ref_in_targeted_fetch_does_not_execute. 14 unit tests pass; ruff clean.
